reasoning: The resolution rests on the 30 days before March 8, 2026 (February 6 to March 8, 2026). ACLED data indicated there were 0 (or ~0) riots over the past 360 days up to Feb 2026. Therefore, the threshold to trigger a YES resolution is 10 x (1 + average) = ~10 riots. French Polynesia is highly stable, with virtually zero historical riots relative to its neighbor New Caledonia. Thorough internet searches in both English and French reveal no violent unrest, riots, or any signs of a major crisis affecting French Polynesia in February or early March 2026. Very minor union/teacher strikes (grèves) occurred, but ACLED strictly distinguishes 'protests' from 'riots'. Since no significant wave of violence is documented, it is virtually impossible for ACLED to record >10 riots in the period.
